entries.title: Sync your real Steam library in one tap
entries.body: No more typing in every game by hand. Open ⋮ → "Sync Steam Library", paste a free Steam API key and your SteamID, and we'll pull your entire owned-games list straight from Steam — with your real playtime included. Preview every game before importing, with already-owned and already-synced titles unchecked for you automatically. Your Steam key is stored only on this device and never leaves it except to talk to Steam directly.

entries.title: Alerts now remember anniversaries and almost-earned trophies
entries.body: The bell icon in the header just got smarter. It will now nudge you about gaming anniversaries ("1 year ago you started Elden Ring") and let you know when you are within a few percent of leveling up a trophy ("92% to Gold Century Club"). And if any alert category is not your thing, tap the new settings gear inside the alerts panel to mute it for good — budget warnings, queue reminders, anniversaries, whatever you want quiet.

entries.title: Genre level-ups now get a celebration
entries.body: Push a genre to a new level — or a whole new rank like Adept or Master — and you'll see a toast pop up celebrating it, with a crown if it's your Main Class. Your Gamer Card (the shareable one) now shows your Main Class badge too, right next to your gamer personality. Also fixed a bug where trophy-earned toasts were silently never showing up — those now fire properly alongside the new genre ones.

entries.title: Time Machine: undo accidental deletes and bad imports
entries.body: Your library now quietly saves itself in the background, and you can save a snapshot manually any time too. Open "Time Machine" from the ⋮ menu to see every snapshot with exactly what's changed since — games added, removed, or edited — and restore any of them with one tap. Accidentally deleted a game? A bulk import went sideways? Just roll back to right before it happened. You'll never lose your library to a mistake again.

entries.title: Level up your favorite genres
entries.body: Every genre you play is now its own character class that levels up the more you play it — hours, finishing games, and rating them all earn XP. Head to the Stats tab to see your rank in each genre (Novice through Legend), your "Main Class" — the genre that defines you as a gamer — and what perks you unlock as you level up. It's a fun new way to see where your gaming energy actually goes, and which genre you've truly mastered versus which ones you just dabble in.

entries.title: Price drops in your buy queue now show up as alerts
entries.body: If a game in your Buy Queue hits the target price you set, or drops to a new low even before reaching that target, it now surfaces in the alerts bell up top — no need to keep checking back manually. There's also a new "Check all prices" button on the Buy Queue tab that refreshes every game's price in one tap, instead of checking each one individually. Tapping a price alert jumps you straight to the Buy Queue.

entries.title: A new bell icon watches your budget, queue, and backlog for you
entries.body: There's now a bell icon up top that quietly keeps an eye on the stuff you'd hate to miss: getting close to (or over) your yearly budget, a game that's been sitting shamefully long in your Up Next queue, a backlog game about to expire, a goal deadline coming up fast, or a play session that's been running for hours. Tap the bell to see everything at a glance, dismiss what you've handled, snooze the rest for a day, or turn on desktop notifications so the important ones reach you even with the tab closed.

entries.title: Start a live timer while you play
entries.body: Hit "Timer" on any game card (or the "Start Live Timer" button in a game's detail sheet) and a Now Playing bar drops in at the bottom of the screen with a running stopwatch — it keeps counting even if you switch tabs or come back later. Pause it, resume it, or tap stop to log the session: the hours are pre-filled from the timer, and you can tag your mood (🔥 Great, 👍 Good, 😐 Meh, 💪 Grind) before saving. No more guessing how long you actually played.

entries.title: See your budget impact before you hit save
entries.body: Set a yearly budget on the Stats tab? Now when you add (or edit) a game's price in the Add Game form, you'll see exactly what it does to your budget — money spent so far this year, what's left after this purchase, and a color-coded bar that turns amber as you approach your limit and red if you'd go over. No more finding out you blew the budget after the fact.

entries.title: Import your library from a CSV or JSON file
entries.body: Got a spreadsheet of games, or an old export from this app? Tap ⋮ then "Import games" to bring it in. Drop in a file or paste CSV text, review a preview of what was found (games already in your library are flagged so you don't accidentally duplicate them), uncheck anything you don't want, and import the rest in one go. Pairs with the existing Export so you've got a full backup-and-restore loop.

entries.title: Solo or social? Find out where your hours actually go
entries.body: When logging a play session, you can now tag who you played with — Solo, Co-op, Online, Couch Co-op, or Streamed. The Stats tab's Analytics panel turns those tags into a Social Gaming Breakdown: a solo-vs-social split of your hours, a per-mode bar, and a quick insight like your go-to co-op game. Tag a few sessions to unlock it.

entries.title: Backlog Triage — make peace with your pile of unplayed games
entries.body: Tap ⋮ then "Backlog Triage" to swipe through the games in your library that actually need a decision — the ones going stale unstarted, or stalled mid-playthrough. One card at a time, you choose: Queue it up next, Let it go (mark abandoned), or Snooze for three weeks. A summary at the end tallies how many hours and dollars you finally made a call on. No more scrolling past the same forgotten games every time you open the app.

entries.title: Set goals and track your progress
entries.body: A new Gaming Goals panel on the Stats tab lets you set personal challenges — finish a number of games, stick to a spending limit, hit an hours target, branch into new genres, clear your backlog, or track any custom metric you like. Watch your progress bar fill in real time, and look back at past goals in a collapsible history once they're done.

entries.title: Play Tonight — find your perfect match in seconds
entries.body: Tap ⋮ then "Play Tonight" and tell the app how much time you have — Quick, Standard, Long, or Marathon. It instantly ranks your unfinished games by chemistry, session-length fit, and current momentum, showing your top pick with a highlighted card and a one-line reason, plus four runner-ups. Tap any result to jump straight into the game's detail sheet and log your session.

entries.title: Compare any two games head to head
entries.body: Open any game's detail sheet, tap the new ⇄ Compare button, and pick a second game from your library. You'll see a full side-by-side breakdown — price, hours, rating, cost-per-hour, ROI, sessions — with a winner highlighted for each category, a radar chart visualising both profiles at once, and an overall verdict showing which game came out on top.

entries.title: What would your library look like with better choices?
entries.body: The new Alternate Reality Simulator in the Stats tab lets you explore four "what if" scenarios: what if you'd skipped impulse buys, never bought games you later abandoned, cleared your backlog, or always bought on sale? Each scenario shows the exact games affected, a before/after stat comparison, and a frank insight about your spending habits.

entries.title: Search your library instantly
entries.body: A search bar now lives at the top of the Games tab. Type a game name, genre, or platform and your list narrows in real time. Works great when you have a big library and just want to find that one game quickly.

entries.title: Updates live here now
entries.body: This is the new What's New panel. When the app changes, you'll see a friendly summary here so you always know what's different.
